understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve the level within the step budget by moving the currently selected piece around the grid (and switching between multiple selectables when needed) to reach/cover the goal tile(s) (+), potentially using pushes and interactions with walls/obstacles to clear a path.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates with 3 pixels per grid cell, so a single move changed x or y by ±3 when it succeeded. MOVE actions attempted to shift only the currently selected piece, but could be blocked by walls/objects; the movement_blocked flags were only hints and not guarantees. If a pushable block was directly adjacent in the move direction, it could be pushed so it moved along with the selected piece. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with an exact object position switched which piece was controlled (as seen when control jumped between [12, 21], [21, 21], and [36, 21]). Walls (#) acted as boundaries/obstacles that could stop movement, though not all wall-like barriers necessarily behaved identically.
